trends.name: Fintech Maturity Shift
trends.description: Financial technology moving from hyper-growth to value creation and profitability as primary metrics. The era of growth-at-all-costs in fintech is structurally over.

signals.name: SVB Collapse Triggering Industry Consolidation
signals.description: Silicon Valley Bank failure in 2023 accelerated fintech rationalization, eliminating weak players and forcing surviving firms toward sustainable business models.

signals.name: Fintech Profitability Entering Mainstream
signals.description: Investor and operator focus shifting decisively from user growth metrics to unit economics and path-to-profit in financial services startups.

trends.name: AI Integration into Strategic Foresight
trends.description: AI copilots and generative tools becoming embedded in professional foresight practice, reshaping how futures are mapped, validated, and communicated.

trends.name: Decentralized Science Emergence
trends.description: DeSci (Decentralized Science) challenging traditional institutional research structures, enabling open-source knowledge as a public good.

trends.name: Climate Crisis as Strategic Forcing Function
trends.description: Climate pressures and global decoupling restructuring both foresight methodology and the scenarios organizations must plan for through 2035.

signals.name: Explosion of AI-Generated Foresight Content
signals.description: Volume of AI-produced trend reports and futures analyses expanding rapidly, creating signal-to-noise challenges for professional foresight practitioners.

signals.name: Hyper-Agile AI Startups Disrupting Incumbents
signals.description: Small AI-native startups outmaneuvering traditional foresight and consulting firms through rapid iteration and lower operational overhead.

scenarios.name: Foresight as Public Infrastructure
scenarios.description: By 2035, strategic foresight tools and outputs become open-source public goods accessible beyond institutional gatekeepers, fundamentally democratizing futures literacy.

trends.name: Age of AI in Financial Services
trends.description: AI transforming banking operations, workforce, and product design at a pace analogous to the digital revolution of the past 25 years, but compressed into years not decades.

trends.name: Generative AI Operational Integration
trends.description: GenAI moving from experimentation to core banking infrastructure, embedding in customer service, risk, compliance, and product development.

signals.name: Only 27% of Banks Ready to Scale GenAI
signals.description: Accenture survey finds most banking institutions acknowledge GenAI's importance but lack the data, talent, and governance infrastructure to operationalize it.

signals.name: AI Perceived as Opportunity Over Threat in Banking
signals.description: Majority of banking executives now frame generative AI as a competitive advantage rather than a workforce threat, reversing sentiment from 2022.

trends.name: Accelerating Rate of Business Change
trends.description: Rate of change affecting businesses rose 183% since 2019 and 33% in the past year alone, driven primarily by technology and climate pressures.

trends.name: Technology as Primary Disruption Driver
trends.description: Technology — led by generative AI — has become the dominant force reshaping organizational strategy, operations, and competitive dynamics across all sectors.

signals.name: 44% of Executives Report GenAI Readiness Gap
signals.description: Nearly half of C-suite leaders say their organizations need more than six months to reach meaningful scale with generative AI deployments.

signals.name: Climate Rising as Second Change Driver
signals.description: Climate appears twice in Accenture's top drivers of business change, indicating its transition from ESG reporting concern to operational and strategic imperative.

trends.name: Human-Centered AI Design
trends.description: Technology shifting from requiring human adaptation to adapting to human behavior, language, and context — a fundamental inversion of the human-machine relationship.

trends.name: AI Agent Ecosystem Proliferation
trends.description: Autonomous AI agents forming interconnected ecosystems capable of taking action across complex multi-step workflows without human intervention at each step.

trends.name: Spatial Computing as Interface Layer
trends.description: Spatial computing technologies enabling new physical-digital interaction surfaces, moving beyond screen-based computing toward ambient and embodied interfaces.

signals.name: Gen AI Producing Human-Like Creative Outputs
signals.description: Generative AI creating scripts, images, and media outputs indistinguishable from human-made work, forcing new frameworks for authenticity and attribution.

signals.name: AI Copilots Becoming Standard Work Tools
signals.description: AI assistants transitioning from novelty to baseline productivity infrastructure in knowledge work environments across industries.

trends.name: AI-Personalized Customer Experience
trends.description: AI enabling real-time hyper-personalization of customer interactions at scale, moving from segment-based to individual-level targeting.

trends.name: Predictive Commerce Integration
trends.description: Shoppable content and predictive personalization collapsing the distance between awareness and purchase across all digital touchpoints.

signals.name: 39% of Consumers Trust AI to Solve Major Problems
signals.description: Consumer confidence in AI capabilities crossing a threshold that enables brands to deploy AI-facing customer experiences without significant friction.

signals.name: Proactive AI Customer Service Emerging
signals.description: Brands beginning to deploy AI that anticipates customer needs before they escalate, shifting from reactive to predictive service models.

trends.name: AI-Driven Food System Transformation
trends.description: Artificial intelligence entering food production, design, and consumption at multiple points simultaneously — from seed genetics to personalized nutrition.

signals.name: AI-Generated Diet Personalization
signals.description: Emergence of AI systems designing individualized dietary regimens based on genetic, microbiome, and lifestyle data, moving nutrition from general guidelines to precision prescriptions.

signals.name: Seed AI Genetics for Food Production
signals.description: AI accelerating crop genetics development, compressing decades-long breeding cycles and enabling faster adaptation to climate stress and nutritional requirements.

trends.name: Attention Scarcity Economy
trends.description: Consumer attention becoming a scarce, contested resource as channel proliferation and content volume outpace human cognitive bandwidth.

trends.name: Short-Termism vs Brand Equity Tension
trends.description: Marketing investment under sustained pressure to demonstrate immediate performance returns, systematically underfunding long-term brand building.

signals.name: Performance Marketing Crowding Out Brand Spend
signals.description: Budget allocation shifting measurably from brand equity campaigns to performance-driven digital advertising as CFOs demand shorter payback periods.

trends.name: AI as Brand Creative Infrastructure
trends.description: AI becoming embedded in brand strategy as creator, storyteller, and personalization engine — moving from campaign tool to core brand operating system.

trends.name: Privacy-First Trust Architecture
trends.description: Consumer demand for transparent data practices becoming a competitive differentiator, with ethical data use shifting from compliance to brand identity.

signals.name: AI Personas in Brand Campaigns
signals.description: Brands deploying AI-powered brand personas and spokespeople (e.g. Virgin Voyages 'Jen AI') as primary customer-facing creative assets.

signals.name: Purpose-Led Brand Initiatives Gaining Traction
signals.description: Ethical and sustainable brand values increasingly resonating with consumers as purchase drivers, moving beyond greenwashing to structural brand commitment.

trends.name: Persistent Inflation Reshaping Consumer Behavior
trends.description: Legacy inflation effects continuing to constrain consumer spending globally despite nominal price stabilization, creating structural caution in household financial decision-making.

trends.name: Geopolitical Disruption of Consumer Markets
trends.description: Geopolitical fragmentation and economic policy divergence creating uneven regional consumer trajectories, breaking the assumption of synchronized global recovery.

signals.name: Global Consumer Spending Intent Declining for 2025
signals.description: AlixPartners survey showing majority of global consumers intending to reduce discretionary spending in 2025, with particularly pronounced caution in developed markets.

signals.name: Unattainable Property Prices Driving Present-Spending
signals.description: Young consumers redirecting money away from long-term saving (unachievable housing) toward present-tense experience spending, reshaping savings behavior.
